text: Self-myofascial release with a foam roll or roller massager increases joint range of motion in the short term without negatively affecting muscle performance.
Foam rolling or roller massage after intense exercise may help attenuate decrements in muscle performance and reduce delayed onset muscle soreness (DOMS).
Short bouts of self-myofascial release performed prior to exercise do not appear to negatively affect muscle performance.
There is currently no consensus on the optimal self-myofascial release program in terms of duration, frequency, or technique due to heterogeneity of methods across studies.
Foam rolling and roller massage may be used as both a pre-exercise warm-up tool to enhance ROM and a post-exercise recovery tool to maintain muscle performance.
